commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the shared agent-core prompt-template argument parser to preserve in-word apostrophes and adds regression coverage for prompt-template argument substitution.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +51. Total +76 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main treats apostrophes as quote delimiters in parseCommandArgs, and the AgentSession prompt path expands templates by default before sending text. I did not run local tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the closing-quote parser path and add one focused regression before maintainer compatibility review.

target.title: fix(agents): keep apostrophes in slash-command prompt-template arguments
target.body:
## Summary

- `parseCommandArgs` (`packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-template-arguments.ts`) expands `$ARGUMENTS` / `$@` / `$1..$N` in user prompt templates and custom slash commands. It does shell-style quote parsing with **no escape and no unbalanced-quote recovery**, so a lone `'` — an ordinary apostrophe in English prose (`user's`, `it's`, `don't`) — opens a quote that runs to the next apostrophe (or end of string). The apostrophe is dropped and the following whitespace is swallowed into one token. The corrupted tokens then feed `$ARGUMENTS` (`args.join(" ")`) and the positional placeholders, so the model **silently receives a mangled instruction** with no error or warning.
  - `/cmd don't it's broken now` + a `[$1][$2]` template collapses to `ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken]` — both apostrophes dropped, placeholders shifted.
- Reach: shipped and default-on. `AgentSession.prompt()` expands templates by default (`expandPromptTemplates ?? true`) and the **expanded text becomes the user message sent to the model**. Templates are real user `.md` files loaded by `loadPromptTemplates` from `<agentDir>/prompts`, `<cwd>/.openclaw/prompts`, and explicit prompt paths.
- Fix: an apostrophe that sits **inside a word** (a word character on both sides — a contraction like `don't`, `it's`, `José's`) is treated as a literal and can neither open nor close a quoted span. Every other quote still behaves as a shell-style delimiter, so balanced spans (`alpha "beta gamma"`) and mid-token spans (`--title="two words"`, `foo='bar baz'`) group exactly as before. Word-character detection is Unicode-aware (`\p{L}`/`\p{N}`), so non-ASCII prose like `José's` is preserved, and an unmatched leading quote (`'review don't fail`) no longer latches onto a later contraction.
- One canonical change in `parseCommandArgs`; both consumers (`expandPromptTemplate` in `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.ts` and `formatPromptTemplateInvocation` in `packages/agent-core`) benefit with no API change. The separate `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (native channel commands) is a different function and is left untouched.

## Tests

- `packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-templates.test.ts`: added cases covering apostrophes preserved in parsed tokens and through `$ARGUMENTS` / positional placeholders; multiple contractions in one input; shell-style quoted spans embedded in a token (`--title="two words"`, `foo='bar baz'`); apostrophes after non-ASCII word characters (`José's don't fail`); and an unmatched leading quote that must not close on a later contraction (`'review don't fail`). The existing quoted/multiline and placeholder-safety cases are unchanged and green.
- `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.test.ts` (`expandPromptTemplate`) unchanged and green.
- Touched suites run locally with the repo runner; type-aware oxlint + oxfmt clean on the touched files.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a user-authored custom slash command / prompt template invoked with ordinary prose containing an apostrophe silently lost the apostrophe (and, for positional `$N` templates, collapsed arguments and shifted later placeholders), so the model received a corrupted instruction with no error or warning. Covers ASCII contractions, multiple contractions in one input, non-ASCII contractions, and an unmatched leading quote.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19, exercising the actual production functions `loadPromptTemplates` + `expandPromptTemplate` — the same expansion path `AgentSession.prompt()` uses to build the outbound user message — over a real on-disk prompt directory (`<agentDir>/prompts/*.md`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote `<tmp>/prompts/review.md` = `Please address: $ARGUMENTS` and `<tmp>/prompts/cmd.md` = `ARG1=[$1] ARG2=[$2] ARG3=[$3] ARG4=[$4]`; loaded them via `loadPromptTemplates({ cwd, agentDir, promptPaths, includeDefaults: false })`; called `expandPromptTemplate(...)` for each input below; ran the script over the **current PR head** (`0710bb1a086`) and, for the before column, over the `origin/main` parser source with the rest of the path identical.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from running the real `expandPromptTemplate` over the on-disk templates on the current PR head `0710bb1a086`:

  ```
  TEMPLATES LOADED: [ 'cmd', 'review' ]
  USER TYPES : /cmd don't it's broken now
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[don't] ARG2=[it's] ARG3=[broken] ARG4=[now]

  USER TYPES : /cmd --title="two words" next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[--title=two words] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd José's don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[José's] ARG2=[don't] ARG3=[fail] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd 'review don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=['review] ARG2=[don't] ARG3=[fail] ARG4=[]
  ```

- Observed result after fix: apostrophes survive end to end across contractions, multiple contractions, non-ASCII prose, and an unmatched leading quote; mid-token shell quoting (`--title="two words"`) still groups into a single argument. The model receives the exact instruction the user typed.
- What was not tested: the separate native-channel `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (a different function, untouched); a fully wired gateway round-trip through a live provider (the proof drives the exact expansion function that produces the user message rather than issuing a model call).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none specific — the expansion is deterministic and reproduced on demand.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same script over the `origin/main` parser (unpatched) corrupts every case except the already-balanced mid-token span:

  ```
  TEMPLATES LOADED: [ 'cmd', 'review' ]
  USER TYPES : /cmd don't it's broken now
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken] ARG3=[now] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd --title="two words" next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[--title=two words] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd José's don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[Josés dont] ARG2=[fail] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd 'review don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[review dont] ARG2=[fail] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]
  ```
target.author: yetval

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
